summary: Iteration 11 — Full end-to-end testing of the NEW Roast Notification System + Focus Mode FocusSession timer rework. Backend pytest 11/11 PASSED (POST /api/session/abandon, GET /api/roasts/check, PUT /api/users/notification-settings, plus regression smoke on auth/habits/shops). Frontend Playwright verified the complete Focus Mode Begin -> FocusSession -> Abandon -> Roast Toast flow end-to-end on the live preview URL. Game Mode regression confirmed (checkbox flow intact, no Begin buttons rendered). Admin Dashboard renders correctly. No critical defects found.

issues: When the user has 0 gems, the abandon toast displays '-0 gems' which is awkward UX. Consider suppressing the gem-deduction toast (or showing 'Streak shield consumed' / 'No gems lost') when data.gems_deducted === 0. Pure cosmetic — backend math is correct (min(30, gems)=0 when gems=0).
The 'Abandon Session' button uses text-white/20 (very low contrast on black background). It is intentionally subtle but borders on accessibility-failing contrast. Consider text-white/40 minimum.

passed_tests: POST /api/session/abandon -> deducts min(30, gems), returns {gems_deducted, roast}, persisted via /auth/me
GET /api/roasts/check returns valid {roasts: [...]} list when roast_enabled = True
PUT /api/users/notification-settings toggles roast_enabled and persists (verified via /auth/me)
Auth login: test@habitrpg.com (Focus) and admin@habitrpg.com (Game) both OK
Frontend Focus Mode: 2 Begin buttons (data-testid='habit-begin-*') rendered for uncompleted habits
Click Begin -> FocusSession opens (data-testid='focus-session'); habit name, session-timer (14:59), abandon-session-btn all rendered
Click Abandon Session -> data-testid='abandon-modal' appears with both keep-going-btn and confirm-abandon-btn
Click Keep Going -> modal closes, focus-session remains active
Click Abandon again -> confirm-abandon-btn fires POST /api/session/abandon; roast toast appears in top-right ('Walked away again. The habit isn't going to build itself.') and focus-session unmounts
Game Mode regression: 0 Begin buttons, checkbox flow intact (data-testid='habit-checkbox-*')
Admin Dashboard renders Overview/Users/Shop tabs with full data (Top XP, Top Streaks, Top Gems)
Mode-based route gating works (Focus user can't access /shop; Game user can't access /focus-shop) — redirects to '/'
RoastListener (top-right toast) also rendered Day's-half-gone roast for admin in Game Mode (background scheduled fire from /api/roasts/check)
action_items: (Minor UX) In /app/frontend/src/components/FocusSession.js line 61: when data.gems_deducted === 0, change toast to 'Session abandoned' or 'Streak shield consumed' instead of '-0 gems'.
(Minor a11y) Increase abandon-session-btn contrast from text-white/20 to at least text-white/40 (FocusSession.js line 181).
(Carry-over from iter_10) ShopPage DecorationsGrid preview cards still don't apply item.css to deco-wrap; cosmetic only.
(Carry-over) Public profile 'Level N -- Name' double-dash on level badge.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/session/abandon (lines 1344-1366): correctly uses min(30, gems) clamp so user gems never go negative; shield decrement only when shields > 0 — good. Returns roast string formatted with mins_elapsed.

server.py /api/roasts/check (lines 1369-1427): respects roast_enabled flag, daily cap of 2 roasts via db.notifications, tracks last_roast_streak_val to avoid repeating streak-broke roast. Solid logic. One nit: when 'roasts' is empty due to no conditions firing, last_roast_streak_val is still overwritten — that's intentional.

FocusSession.js: handleAbandon uses startTimeRef to compute mins_elapsed but startTimeRef is set in useRef initializer to Date.now() at mount — works because component unmounts on abandon, so no stale-ref issue.
FocusSession.js setInterval uses setSecondsLeft(prev => prev - 1) with stale-closure-safe functional update — good.

RoastNotification.js: RoastNotification polls /api/roasts/check every 5 min and stores dismissed in Set — fine. RoastListener uses window event 'phase-roast' for in-process toasts (used by FocusSession.handleAbandon) — works without prop drilling.

HomePage.js: AnimatePresence around FocusSession is mode-gated `!isGameMode` — correctly prevents Game Mode users from ever seeing the timer; HabitCard branches isGameMode for Begin vs checkbox render.
Code smell: FocusSession.js imports X from lucide-react but never uses it (only re-imported below). Safe to remove unused import.
seed_data_creation: None. Focus test user already had 2 morning habits including TEST_gems_habit_v8 from prior iterations. Test user gems went from 0 -> 0 (clamped). Admin notification_settings.roast_enabled toggled off then back on; ended in roast_enabled=True (matches pre-test state).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Roast Notification + Focus Session Abandon features are fully wired backend->frontend and verified end-to-end. Backend endpoints: POST /api/session/abandon, GET /api/roasts/check, PUT /api/users/notification-settings. Frontend components: /app/frontend/src/components/FocusSession.js (full-screen timer + abandon modal) and /app/frontend/src/components/RoastNotification.js (RoastListener uses CustomEvent('phase-roast'), RoastNotification polls every 5 min). HomePage HabitCard renders Begin button only when !isGameMode && !habit.completed_today. Two minor UX nits open (see action_items). Focus test user (test@habitrpg.com) has 0 gems — for happy-path full-30-deduction regression, grant gems via DB or use a freshly seeded TEST_ user with gems>=30. All previous iterations 1-10 features still pass smoke (auth, habits, shops both modes, admin).
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. Two minor UX/a11y suggestions documented above.
